titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Theodore L. Badger Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 09:02

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, writings, printed matter, photographs, and minutes and reports relating primarily to Theodore Badger's term on the board of directors of the International Grenfell Association.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Mrs. Theodore L. Badger, 1984-1986.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Theodore L. Badger Papers (MS 1385). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Theodore L. Badger Papers contain material documenting Dr. Badger's affiliation with the International Grenfell Association (I.G.A.). Theodore Badger, colleague and friend of Sir Wilfred Grenfell, served on the Board of Directors of the I.G.A. from 1948-1975 and was chairman from 1952-1953. The papers consist of correspondence, writings, printed matter, photographs, and I.G.A. minutes and reports. Dr. Badger's correspondence with Sir Wilfred and Lady Ann Grenfell is of particular interest.
The Theodore L. Badger Papers were donated to Yale University by Mrs. Badger in 1984.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

did.unittitle: Articles from I.G.A. publication Among the Deep Sea Fishes
Biographical sketch of Wilfred T. Grenfell
Profiles of individuals associated with the Grenfell Mission in Labrador and photographs
Report: "The Use of Transparent Cellulose Film for Insulation"
Report to the Board of Directors
Report on the Preliminary Survey of the International Grenfell Association
Reports on the future of I.G.A.
Report of the Meeting at the Committee on Northern Health and Medicine in Newfoundland and Labrador
Annual Report of the Executive Director
Annual Report: Northwest River Hospital and Nursing Stations; correspondence
Supporting Associations: New England Grenfell Association
By-laws, rules, regulations: St. Anthony Hospital

Envelope no. 2: Sir Wilfred Grenfell, fish drying, Red Bay gardens (6)

Envelope no. 6: Sir Wilfred Grenfell, Dr. Curtis, St. Anthony's Hospital (1)

Envelope no. 13: Sir Wilfred with boy on crutches (1)
